purpose: Educational report on mining and cryptocurrency concepts
disclaimer: FOR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY - NO ACTUAL MINING OCCURS

proof_of_work.name: Proof of Work (PoW)
proof_of_work.description: A consensus mechanism where participants solve complex mathematical puzzles to validate transactions and create new blocks.
proof_of_work.how_it_works: Miners compete to solve cryptographic puzzles. The first to solve gets to create the next block and receives rewards. The puzzle difficulty adjusts based on network hash rate.
proof_of_work.security_model: Computational security - requires 51% of network hash rate to attack

proof_of_stake.name: Proof of Stake (PoS)
proof_of_stake.description: A consensus mechanism where validators are chosen based on the amount of cryptocurrency they hold and are willing to 'stake' as collateral.
proof_of_stake.how_it_works: Validators lock up (stake) their cryptocurrency. They are randomly selected to validate blocks based on their stake amount. Validators receive rewards for honest behavior and lose stake for malicious behavior.
proof_of_stake.security_model: Economic security - requires 51% of staked tokens to attack

proof_of_authority.name: Proof of Authority (PoA)
proof_of_authority.description: A consensus mechanism where validators are pre-approved based on their identity and reputation.
proof_of_authority.how_it_works: Pre-approved validators take turns creating blocks. Validators are identified and held accountable for their actions through their real-world identity.
proof_of_authority.security_model: Reputation-based security - relies on validator identity and reputation

randomx.name: RandomX
randomx.description: A proof-of-work algorithm designed to be CPU-friendly and ASIC-resistant, used by Monero.
randomx.mathematical_basis: Uses random code execution to create a virtual machine that runs different programs for each block. The algorithm is designed to be memory-intensive and CPU-optimized.
randomx.hardware_requirements: High-end CPUs with large L3 cache, 2GB+ RAM per thread, fast memory bandwidth
randomx.difficulty_adjustment: Adjusts every block based on the previous 720 blocks (approximately 2 hours)
randomx.security_features: ASIC-resistant, memory-hard, CPU-optimized, random program execution
randomx.energy_consumption: Moderate - CPU-intensive but more efficient than GPU mining

ethash.name: Ethash
ethash.description: A proof-of-work algorithm used by Ethereum (pre-merge) designed to be memory-hard and ASIC-resistant.
ethash.mathematical_basis: Uses a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) that grows over time. The algorithm requires large amounts of memory to store the DAG, making it difficult for ASICs to gain significant advantages.
ethash.hardware_requirements: GPUs with 4GB+ VRAM, high memory bandwidth, moderate computational power
ethash.difficulty_adjustment: Adjusts every block based on network hash rate and target block time
ethash.energy_consumption: High - GPU-intensive with large memory requirements

kawpow.name: KawPow
kawpow.description: A proof-of-work algorithm used by Ravencoin designed to be ASIC-resistant and GPU-friendly.
kawpow.mathematical_basis: Uses a combination of Keccak256 and ProgPow algorithms. It includes random program generation and memory access patterns to prevent ASIC optimization.
kawpow.hardware_requirements: Modern GPUs with 4GB+ VRAM, good memory bandwidth, moderate computational power
kawpow.difficulty_adjustment: Adjusts every block based on network hash rate
kawpow.security_features: ASIC-resistant, GPU-optimized, random program generation, memory-hard
kawpow.energy_consumption: High - GPU-intensive with complex computational patterns

sha256.name: SHA-256
sha256.description: A cryptographic hash function used by Bitcoin and many other cryptocurrencies.
sha256.mathematical_basis: Uses the SHA-256 hash function to create a puzzle that miners must solve. The puzzle involves finding a nonce that produces a hash below a target value.
sha256.hardware_requirements: ASICs (Application-Specific Integrated Circuits), specialized mining hardware
sha256.difficulty_adjustment: Adjusts every 2016 blocks (approximately 2 weeks) based on network hash rate
sha256.energy_consumption: Very high - requires specialized ASIC hardware

hierarchical_deterministic.description: A wallet that generates a tree of key pairs from a single seed, allowing for easy backup and multiple addresses.
hierarchical_deterministic.key_generation: Uses a master seed (usually 12-24 words) to derive a master private key. Child keys are generated using BIP-32/BIP-44 standards.
hierarchical_deterministic.address_derivation: Addresses are derived from public keys using cryptographic functions. Each address is unique and can be generated deterministically.
hierarchical_deterministic.security_features: Single seed backup, key derivation, address isolation, deterministic generation
hierarchical_deterministic.backup_methods: Seed phrase backup, hardware wallet storage, encrypted backups
hierarchical_deterministic.educational_diagram: Master Seed → Master Private Key → Child Private Keys → Public Keys → Addresses

multisignature.description: A wallet that requires multiple private keys to authorize transactions, providing enhanced security.
multisignature.key_generation: Multiple private keys are generated independently. A quorum (e.g., 2-of-3) is required to sign transactions.
multisignature.address_derivation: Addresses are created using multiple public keys. Transactions require signatures from the required number of private keys.
multisignature.security_features: Distributed key management, threshold signatures, reduced single point of failure
multisignature.backup_methods: Multiple key backups, hardware wallet distribution, geographic distribution
multisignature.educational_diagram: Key 1 + Key 2 + Key 3 → Multisig Address → Requires 2-of-3 signatures

hardware_wallet.description: A physical device that stores private keys securely and signs transactions offline.
hardware_wallet.key_generation: Private keys are generated within the secure hardware environment and never leave the device.
hardware_wallet.address_derivation: Addresses are derived within the hardware wallet and displayed for verification.
hardware_wallet.security_features: Offline key storage, tamper-resistant hardware, PIN protection, backup recovery
hardware_wallet.backup_methods: Seed phrase backup, multiple hardware wallets, secure storage
hardware_wallet.educational_diagram: Hardware Device → Secure Key Storage → Offline Signing → Transaction Broadcast

poolin.name: Poolin
poolin.description: One of the largest Bitcoin mining pools, offering various cryptocurrency mining options.
poolin.stratum_protocol: Uses Stratum protocol for communication between miners and pool servers. Supports both TCP and SSL connections.
fee_structures: Variable fees based on pool size and features
poolin.educational_analysis: Large pools can centralize mining power, potentially threatening network decentralization. Pool operators have significant influence over network decisions.

f2pool.name: F2Pool
f2pool.description: A major mining pool supporting multiple cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and others.
f2pool.stratum_protocol: Implements Stratum protocol with additional features for better efficiency and reliability.
f2pool.educational_analysis: F2Pool has been involved in network governance decisions, demonstrating the influence of large mining pools on cryptocurrency networks.

antpool.name: AntPool
antpool.description: A mining pool operated by Bitmain, one of the largest ASIC manufacturers.
antpool.stratum_protocol: Advanced Stratum implementation with features for ASIC optimization and monitoring.
antpool.educational_analysis: AntPool's connection to Bitmain creates potential conflicts of interest and centralization concerns in the mining ecosystem.

educational_disclaimer.purpose: This module is for educational purposes only
educational_disclaimer.no_mining: NO ACTUAL MINING OPERATIONS ARE PERFORMED
educational_disclaimer.no_revenue: NO REVENUE OR PROFIT IS GENERATED
educational_disclaimer.theoretical_only: All calculations and examples are theoretical
educational_disclaimer.defensive_focus: Focus is on understanding for defensive analysis
educational_disclaimer.compliance: All activities comply with legal and ethical guidelines

educational_objectives: Learn about mining algorithms and their characteristics
Learn about mining pools and their role
defensive_applications: Monitor system resources for mining indicators
Configure security tools to detect mining software
Develop incident response procedures for mining incidents
